speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Oh thank God you're here! Everything is encrypted! The whole network! We can't access ANYTHING!

choices.text: Take a deep breath, Dave. Walk me through what happened.
When did you first notice the encryption?

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: OK, OK... so my plant manager called me at 5:47 AM. Said all the files on the shared drive had weird extensions. Then he found a note demanding Bitcoin.

choices.text: Do you still have that ransom note? I'll need to see it.
Has anyone touched the systems since then?

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Uh, my plant manager noticed around 5:47 this morning. But honestly, I don't know when it actually started. Could've been overnight for all I know.

choices.text: That's helpful. Overnight attacks are common. Did you have anyone working late last night?
We'll need to check the event logs to determine the actual timeline.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: NO! My lawyer said absolutely not. But... the ransom is 2 Bitcoin. That's what, like $80,000? Our manufacturing line is losing $50,000 a DAY being down...

choices.text: I strongly advise against paying. There's no guarantee they'll decrypt, and it funds more attacks.
Let's focus on the investigation first. We may be able to recover without paying.
That's between you and your lawyer. My job is to find out how they got in.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Well... our IT guy, Mike, he rebooted the file server and the domain controller. He said he was 'trying to fix it.' I told him to stop touching things after your firm said to preserve everything.

choices.text: That's unfortunate but not uncommon. We can still work with what's there. Did Mike do anything else?
Rebooting may have destroyed volatile memory evidence. We need to talk to Mike directly.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: I think... all of them? At least the file server and a bunch of workstations. Maybe 15-20 machines? We have about 50 employees. Our email still works because it's Office 365.

choices.text: Small silver lining on the email. Can you get me a network diagram?
We'll need to check each machine. Do you have an IT inventory?

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: No, everyone leaves by 6 PM usually. But... actually, John in accounting said he got a weird email yesterday about an invoice. He might have clicked something. Oh God.

choices.text: That's very helpful. Can you get me access to John's email and workstation?
Don't panic. Phishing is the most common initial access vector. We'll check it out.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Yeah, it's on every encrypted machine. Says 'DarkLock' something. Wants 2 Bitcoin sent to some wallet address. Gives us 72 hours or the price doubles.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: You're right, you're right. My lawyer said the same thing. Just... please find out how they got in so this doesn't happen again. I can't go through this twice.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Really? You think we can recover without paying? That would be... that would be amazing. What do you need from us?

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: He said he ran some antivirus scan thing but it didn't find anything. Then he 'restored from backup' but the backup was encrypted too. Poor guy, he's been up since 5 AM.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Sure, I'll get him on a call. He's... he's pretty shaken up. He keeps saying 'I should have had better backups.' He's blaming himself.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Absolutely. Whatever you need. I'll tell everyone to cooperate fully. Just... find the bastards who did this.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: OK. OK, I feel a little better knowing this is... normal? Is that the right word? God, that's depressing that this is normal.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Network diagram? Uh... I don't think we have one. Mike might know the setup. We have a file server, a domain controller, and a web server that hosts our company website.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: IT inventory? Like a spreadsheet of all our computers? I... don't think we have that. Mike might have something on a sticky note somewhere.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Event logs? I don't know what those are. You'll have to talk to Mike about the technical stuff. I can get you remote access to the network.

speaker: You
text: I'll need VPN access to your network, admin credentials for the affected systems, and any logs from your firewall. Can you arrange that?

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Done. I'll have Mike send you everything. Just... keep me updated, OK? My board is meeting tomorrow and they're going to want answers.

speaker: You
text: Good. I have enough to start. I'll begin analyzing the evidence and get back to you with initial findings.

speaker: Dave Morrison (CEO)
text: Thank you. Seriously. I haven't slept since 5 AM and my wife is going to kill me if I don't come home soon. Call me anytime - literally anytime - if you find something.

speaker: You
text: That's exactly what we're here for. Let me start the investigation. I'll have preliminary findings within a few hours.
